title: Split login page into separate Admin and Customer entry points

requirements.text: Create a dedicated landing/login selection page that presents two clearly labeled entry points: 'Admin Login' and 'Customer Login', allowing users to choose their portal before proceeding.

quotes: we just create before that on login page here we have to split one admin and customer page

acceptanceCriteria: A login selection page is shown as the default landing route
Two distinct buttons or sections are visible: one for Admin and one for Customer
Clicking 'Admin Login' navigates to the admin authentication flow
Clicking 'Customer Login' navigates to the customer portal login form

requirements.text: Restore the working admin login flow from Version 28: the Admin login page must use Internet Identity authentication, verify admin role via the backend actor, and redirect to the admin dashboard on success. Display a clear error if the authenticated principal is not an admin.

quotes: please understand version 28 is everything is ok that is admin login
Keep the existing admin login flow as it was in Version 28

acceptanceCriteria: Admin Login page shows an Internet Identity login button
After authentication, the backend isAdmin check is called
Successful admin authentication redirects to the admin dashboard
Non-admin principals see a clear 'Access denied' or 'Not authorized' message
Admin session persists across page refreshes

requirements.text: Create a dedicated Customer Login page where customers authenticate using their username and password credentials managed in the backend CustomerAccount store. On success, store the customer session and redirect to the Customer Portal.

quotes: we just create before that on login page here we have to split one admin and customer page

acceptanceCriteria: Customer Login page displays username and password input fields
Submitting valid credentials authenticates the customer via the backend loginCustomer call
Successful login stores the customer session (via CustomerAuthContext) and redirects to the customer portal
Invalid credentials display a user-friendly error message
Customer session persists in sessionStorage across page refreshes

requirements.text: Update the app routing so that the root path ('/') renders the new login selection page. Protect the admin panel routes to redirect unauthenticated or non-admin users back to the admin login page. Protect the customer portal route to redirect unauthenticated customers back to the customer login page.

quotes: Add a separate Customer login section or page
The main login page should clearly separate Admin vs. Customer access

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to '/' shows the login selection page
Navigating directly to an admin route without authentication redirects to admin login
Navigating directly to the customer portal without a customer session redirects to customer login
Both portals are fully inaccessible without proper authentication

constraints: Do not change the backend authentication logic or data model for admin or customer accounts
Preserve the CustomerAuthContext session management in frontend/src/context/CustomerAuthContext.tsx
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Adding new admin or customer account types or roles
Changing the admin dashboard or customer portal feature set beyond fixing login access
Adding social or third-party authentication methods
Redesigning the overall visual theme of the app
